response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his background is almost entirely in web, mobile, and application development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, React). The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in his history. Brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and low-latency DLang services are the closest touchpoints, but they fall far short of principal-level silicon performance architecture.
strengths: Long, consistent software engineering career (15+ years) meeting the experience bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Some systems-level C/C++ and hardware-adjacent exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware-software co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or accelerator optimization background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack product engineering, not performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Missing SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture experience
No AI accelerator or deep-learning performance background
